calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ANKITA KHANNA's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's unopposed demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the ground that the complaint is so uncertain that it does not fairly apprise defendant of what is claimed against her. A demurrer for uncertainty will rarely lie and is strictly construed. (See Khoury v. Maly's of California, Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Here, however, the complaint refers to defendant Khanna as the plaintiff at page 5, and it is not clear from the face of the complaint what her role is in the auto accident alleged to have occurred. Plaintiff has leave to amend within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
